title: Data Security
summary: 9618 AS — threats to data, access control, encryption, and backup strategies for confidentiality and availability.
learningObjectives: Describe threats: hacking, malware, phishing, and social engineering.
Explain authentication, authorisation, and access levels.
Outline symmetric and asymmetric encryption at a high level.
Evaluate backup, disaster recovery, and physical security measures.

simpleExplanation.title: Protecting data assets
simpleExplanation.summary: Data security combines technical controls (encryption, firewalls) with procedural ones (password policy, training). CIA triad: confidentiality, integrity, availability.
steps: Confidentiality: only authorised users read data — encryption, access rights.
Integrity: data not altered without permission — checksums, audit logs.
Availability: systems reachable when needed — backups, redundancy.
Defence in depth: multiple layers — network, application, user education.

sections.content: **Data security** (6.1) pairs with **data integrity** (6.2) on Paper 1. Cambridge often asks you to **compare measures** or **justify** a policy for a given scenario.

items: Firewall filters traffic; antivirus detects known malware signatures
Symmetric key shared; asymmetric uses public/private key pairs (TLS)
Regular off-site backups protect against ransomware and hardware failure
